title: Technically and Economically Unfeasible' | Repsol Scraps Up-to-200MW Green Hydrogen Project
content:
Spanish oil company Repsol has reportedly scrapped plans to build an up-to-200MW green hydrogen project in the city of Puertollano, Spain, after feasibility studies showed that it would be too costly and technically challenging to build.

Repsol and its Hydric co-developer, renewables firm RIC Energy, took the decision following the completion of studies that revealed the â€œtechnical and economic unfeasibilityâ€ of the scheme, Repsol confirmed today.

The pair had envisaged building a 30MW electrolyser at the site of the former Sevillana coal-fired power plant in 2026, and had at one point said they would scale up the plant to 200MW. However, Repsol told Hydrogen Insight that the partners had only been considering a 100MW version.
author: Analysts Account
